title: Goniopteris imbricata
taxonomy: Goniopteris imbricata is classified under the division Pteridophyta, the class Polypodiopsida, the order Polypodiales, and the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Goniopteris. This plant is native to tropical regions of the world and has been found in various parts of Asia and Central and South America. Further classification includes the family being a part of the subfamily Thelypteridoideae. The classification is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics.

In some parts of the world, it is used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. intro: Goniopteris imbricata, also known as the interlocking shield fern, is a species of fern native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Goniopteris imbricata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
